patch.value:
## Defect
Structured dependency edges whose `id` resolves to no existing item are invisible to every layer of the system, and the net effect is a SILENT UNBLOCK of work the author intended to be blocked.

Verified in a clean sandbox on v2026.7.10:
1. `pm create task "dep to ghost" --dep "id=pm-zzzz,kind=blocked_by"` — accepted, edge stored, no warning.
2. `pm validate --json` — ok:true, zero referential warnings. Root cause: both graph builders silently drop unresolvable ids — `src/cli/commands/validate.ts:1189` (`!activeItemIds.has(dependencyId) → continue`) for the dependency-cycle graph, and the `canonicalIdByLowercase.get(...)` miss in `buildLifecycleParentGraph` for parents.
3. `pm next --json` — the ghost-blocked item is counted `ready` and offered as a candidate (summary ready:2 for 2 items). The nonexistent blocker is treated as satisfied.

## Why it matters
- The pre-PR#499 colon-shorthand bug (pm-zazb) historically wrote malformed `pm-related:*` dep ids in the wild; repos carrying that era's data have exactly these edges, and today nothing will ever tell them.
- A single-character typo in `--dep id=` inverts the semantics of `blocked_by`: instead of blocking, it releases the item into the actionable pool. For multi-agent fleets picking work via pm next, this dispatches work whose preconditions are unmet — the opposite of the graph's contract, with no error at write time, no warning at validate time, and no trace at read time.
- Contrast: parent references ARE fail-fast at mutation time (`pm update --parent <nonexistent>` is rejected, pm-z9e9), so deps are the inconsistent surface. A validator-side parent-drift check (stale parent pointing at a since-deleted id) can ride along in the same warning family for symmetry, though the current live graph has zero instances (full-store sweep 2026-07-10: 1708 items, all parent pointers and all structured dep ids resolve except prose mentions).

## Design note for the mutation side
Hard-rejecting nonexistent dep ids at create/update would break legitimate forward-referencing in scripted flows (creating a graph in reverse order). Options: (a) warn on stderr + accept, (b) `--strict-deps` opt-in rejection, (c) accept + rely on validate. The validator warning is the non-negotiable half; the mutation-side choice is the implementer's, but it must be an explicit decision recorded on this item.
Dangling dependency references: accepted at create/update, skipped by validate, and treated as satisfied by pm next (silent unblock)

pm validate emits a dedicated warning (with item ids and the dangling dep ids) for structured dependency edges whose id resolves to no item; a typo'd blocker id can never again yield an actionable pm next candidate without a diagnostic surfacing somewhere; mutation-time behavior for nonexistent dep ids is decided (warn vs reject vs --strict flag) and documented; tests cover create, update, validate, and next paths; remediation hint tells users to repair with --replace-deps

author: codex-root
message: Start broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery bundle; duplicate-safe all-status orientation complete

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start: active in the broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery PR. Live all-status duplicate check, full item metadata, closed prerequisite lineage, GitHub issues, security alerts, dependency audit, release state, and Sentry issue surface were reviewed before claiming. This item shares CLI/SDK contracts, recovery envelopes, and end-to-end verification with the seven sibling items in the bundle.

patch.value: Kept missing blocker IDs unresolved and added dependency-reference validation with remediation hints.
Dangling parent, scalar blocker, and structured dependency references cannot silently unblock work.
Actionability blocks dangling references and validate reports sorted affected rows plus replace-deps remediation.

value.author: codex-wide-pm-sdk
value.text: Verification: pnpm release:gates passed build/typecheck/static/docstring ratchets/docs/context/security, 100/100/100/100 coverage with 5,524 tests, packed npx smoke, and 88-command package dogfood; isolated installed-tarball acceptance created two items, atomically claimed one, accepted comments --body, detected one dangling dependency, and imported PmClient. Live Sentry/telemetry: 0 blocking critical/high, 0.34% finish error rate, zero missing error-code rows. pm health: all 7 checks ok; history drift: 0 across 1,709 items.

patch.value: Implemented and verified in the atomic work-selection, dependency-integrity, recovery, alias, and telemetry bundle.

author: codex-wide-pm-sdk
message: Greptile follow-up: repair invalid second dangling-dependency test fixture and prove both independent rows are retained.

value.author: codex-wide-pm-sdk
value.text: Greptile follow-up resolved: the second fixture used unsupported dependency kind relates_to, so its update command did not persist. Replaced it with a valid blocked_by edge; focused validate suite passes 88/88 and now proves two distinct dangling rows survive sorting and deduplication.

patch.value: Implemented, verified, and corrected the multi-row dangling-reference regression fixture after Greptile review.
